subject: ap-chemistry
title: Acid-Base Titration & Buffer Chemistry
stimulus:
1. A student reacts 0.300 g of methyl salicylate (C $H_2O$ ) with a stoichiometric amount of a strong base. This
8 8 3
product is then acidified to produce salicylic acid crystals (HC $H_2O$ ).
7 5 3

parts.prompt:
For every 1 mole of C H O (molar mass 152.15 g/mol) reactant used, 1 mole of salicylic acid crystals
8 8 3
(HC $H_2O$ , molar mass 138.12 g/mol) is produced. Calculate the maximum mass, in grams, of HC $H_2O$
7 5 3 7 5 3
that could be produced in this reaction.

As part of the experimental procedure to purify the HC $H_2O$ crystals after the reaction is complete, the crystals
7 5 3
are filtered from the reaction mixture, rinsed with distilled water, and dried. Some physical properties of
HC $H_2O$ are given in the following table.
7 5 3
Properties of Salicylic Acid (HC $H_2O$ )
7 5 3
Melting point 159°C
Solubility in $H_2O$ at 25° C 2.2 g/L
Specific heat capacity 1.17 J/( g · ° C)
Heat of fusion 27.1 kJ/mol

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

parts.prompt:

is higher than that of methyl salicylate.

parts.prompt:
The student performs a second titration, this time titrating 20.0 mL of a 0.0100 M benzoic acid solution with
0.0200 M NaOH. Sketch the curve that would result from this titration of benzoic acid on the following graph,
which already shows the original curve from the titration of 20.0 mL of 0.0100 M salicylic acid. The initial
pH of the benzoic acid solution is 3.11.
rubric_criteria: For a curve that shows a correct starting and half-equivalence point, consistent with part (g): 1 point
The curve starts at pH ≈ 3.11 and passes through the pK calculated in part (g) at 5 mL .
For a curve that shows the correct equivalence point: 1 point
